sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved his memorial.

sentences.zh: 充即移書光祿勳、中黃門，逮名近臣侍中諸當詣北軍者，移劾門衛，禁止無令得出入宮殿。

translations.literal: Chong immediately transferred documents to the superintendent of the imperial household and the palace yellow gates, arresting by name close ministers and attendants-in-waiting and all who should go to the northern army, transferring impeachment to gate guards, forbidding them and not allowing them to obtain exit-entry of palaces and halls.
translations.idiomatic: He sent writs to the superintendent of the household and the yellow-gate eunuchs, listing every minister and attendant bound for the northern camp, and ordered the gate wardens to lock the palace so none could slip in or out.

sentences.zh: 於是貴戚子弟惶恐，皆見上叩頭求哀，願得入錢贖罪。

translations.literal: Thereupon noble young men of the outer families were terrified, all saw the sovereign knocking heads begging pity, wishing to be allowed to enter cash to redeem crime.
translations.idiomatic: The great families panicked, threw themselves before the emperor, and offered to buy their way off the conscription lists with gold.

sentences.zh: 上許之，令各以秩次輸錢北軍，凡數千萬。

translations.literal: The sovereign permitted it, ordered each according to rank order to deliver cash to the northern army, altogether several ten millions.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed: each man might commute his service by paying into the northern-army fund according to rank, until several tens of millions had poured in.

sentences.zh: 上以充忠直，奉法不阿，所言中意。

translations.literal: The sovereign because Chong was loyal and straight, upheld law without partiality, his words hit the sovereign's intent.
translations.idiomatic: The court decided that Jiang Chong was upright, unbending in the law, and always spoke the emperor's mind.

sentences.zh: 充出，逢館陶長公主行馳道中。

translations.literal: Chong went out, met the elder Princess of Guantao traveling in the expressway.
translations.idiomatic: Leaving an audience, Jiang Chong met the elder Princess of Guantao racing her carriage down the imperial express lane.

sentences.zh: 充呵問之，公主曰：「有太后詔。」

translations.literal: Chong shouted and questioned her; the princess said: 'There is the empress dowager's edict.'
translations.idiomatic: He halted her with a shout; she answered that she carried the empress dowager's warrant.

sentences.zh: 充曰：「獨公主得行，車騎皆不得。」

translations.literal: Chong said: 'Only the princess may go; chariots and riders all may not.'
translations.idiomatic: "The edict covers you alone," said Jiang Chong. "Your escort may not use this road."

sentences.zh: 盡劾沒入宮。

translations.literal: He impeached all and confiscated them into the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He impounded every carriage and driver and sent them to the palace stores.

sentences.zh: 後充從上甘泉，逢太子家使乘車馬行馳道中，充以屬吏。

translations.literal: Later Chong followed the sovereign to Ganquan, met the heir's household envoy riding chariot and horses in the expressway; Chong handed them to officials.
translations.idiomatic: Later, riding with the emperor to Ganquan, he caught the crown prince's household steward speeding along the reserved track and turned him over to the law.

sentences.zh: 太子聞之，使人謝充曰：「非愛車馬，誠不欲令上聞之，以教敕亡素者。

translations.literal: The heir heard it, sent men to thank Chong, saying: 'It is not that I love chariot and horses; truly I did not wish to let the sovereign hear it, thereby exposing that instruction and discipline have never been regular.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince sent messengers to apologize: "I am not hoarding carriages for myself; I only hoped to spare my father the shame that his heir was never drilled in routine discipline."

sentences.zh: 唯江君寬之！」

translations.literal: Only Lord Jiang, be lenient!'
translations.idiomatic: I beg you, Lord Jiang, to let this pass."

sentences.zh: 充不聽，遂白奏。

translations.literal: Chong did not listen, thereupon reported in white memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Chong refused and filed an open memorial.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「人臣當如是矣。」

translations.literal: The sovereign said: 'A minister ought to be thus.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "That is how a minister should behave."

sentences.zh: 大見信用，威震京師。

translations.literal: He was greatly trusted and employed; awe shook the capital.
translations.idiomatic: From that day Jiang Chong was trusted as few men were, and his name terrified the capital.

sentences.zh: 遷為水衡都尉，宗族、知友多得其力者。

translations.literal: He was promoted to superintendent of waters and parks; clan and knowing friends many obtained his power's help.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to superintendent of waters and parks, and half his kinsmen and friends rode his coattails to office.

sentences.zh: 久之，坐法免。

translations.literal: After a long time, he sat on law and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In time he broke the law and was stripped of rank.

sentences.zh: 會陽陵硃安世告丞相公孫賀子太僕敬聲為巫蠱事，連及陽石、諸邑公主，賀父子皆坐誅。

translations.literal: It happened that Zhu Anshi of Yangling reported Chancellor Gongsun He's son the grand coachman Jingsheng for witchcraft affair, implicating Princess Yangshi and Princess Zhuyi; He father and son both sat and were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Then Zhu Anshi of Yangling denounced Grand Coachman Gongsun Jingsheng, son of Chancellor Gongsun He, for witchcraft, dragging in Princesses Yangshi and Zhuyi; both father and son died under the law.

sentences.zh: 語在《賀傳》。

translations.literal: The words are in the 'Biography of He.'
translations.idiomatic: The full account stands in Gongsun He's biography.

sentences.zh: 後上幸甘泉，疾病，充見上年老，恐晏駕後為太子所誅，因是為奸，奏言上疾祟在巫蠱。

translations.literal: Later the sovereign visited Ganquan, was ill; Chong saw the sovereign's age was great, feared after the imperial carriage's late departure he would be executed by the heir, thereupon acted wickedly, memorialized saying the sovereign's illness's baleful influence lay in witchcraft.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor fell ill at Ganquan, Jiang Chong saw his gray hair and feared the crown prince would execute him at the next succession, so he invented a plot: the sickness, he claimed, came from witchcraft.

sentences.zh: 於是上以充為使者治巫蠱。

translations.literal: Thereupon the sovereign appointed Chong envoy to treat witchcraft.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor named him special commissioner for witchcraft cases.

sentences.zh: 充將胡巫掘地求偶人，捕蠱及夜祠，視鬼，染污令有處，輒收捕驗治，燒鐵鉗灼，強服之。

translations.literal: Chong led Hu shamans digging earth seeking doll figures, capturing gu and night sacrifices, viewing ghosts, smearing filth to make marks, then arresting, examining, treating, burning iron tongs and searing, forcing confession.
translations.idiomatic: He marched foreign shamans through the capital, dug up yards for straw dolls, raided midnight shrines, smeared false evidence, then arrested, tortured with red-hot irons, and extorted confessions.

sentences.zh: 民轉相誣以巫蠱，吏輒劾以大逆亡道，坐而死者前後數萬人。

translations.literal: The people in turn falsely accused each other of witchcraft; officials then impeached them for great rebellion and lacking the Way; those who sat and died were several ten thousands front and rear.
translations.idiomatic: Neighbors denounced neighbors until clerks could pin great treason on anyone; tens of thousands died in the witch hunt.

sentences.zh: 是時，上春秋高，疑左右皆為蠱祝詛，有與亡，莫敢訟其冤者。

translations.literal: At this time the sovereign's springs and autumns were high; he suspected those at his side all performed gu prayers and curses; some guilty some innocent—none dared sue their wrong.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was old and suspicious: he believed everyone near him worked curses in the dark, and no victim dared plead innocence.

sentences.zh: 充既知上意，因言宮中有蠱氣，先治後宮希幸夫人，以次及皇后，遂掘蠱於太子宮，得桐木人。

translations.literal: Chong already knew the sovereign's intent, thereupon said there was gu vapor in the palace, first treated the rear palace's rarely favored ladies, proceeding in order to the empress, then dug gu in the heir's palace, obtained a paulownia wood man.
translations.idiomatic: Reading the emperor's fear, Jiang Chong announced a miasma of witchcraft inside the walls, searched the neglected concubines first, then the empress, and finally turned up a wooden doll buried in the heir's garden.

sentences.zh: 太子懼，不能自明，收充，自臨斬之。

translations.literal: The heir was afraid, could not clear himself, arrested Chong, personally attended beheading him.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince could not clear his name in terror, seized Jiang Chong, and cut off his head with his own hand.

sentences.zh: 罵曰「趙虜！

translations.literal: He cursed, saying: 'Zhao captive!'
translations.idiomatic: He shouted, "You Zhao dog!"

sentences.zh: 亂乃國王父子不足邪！

translations.literal: Throwing into chaos your state's king and father-son is not enough!'
translations.idiomatic: Was it not enough to wreck your own king and crown prince in Zhao?"

sentences.zh: 乃復亂吾父子也！」

translations.literal: And again you throw our father and son into chaos!'
translations.idiomatic: Must you now tear the Han father and son apart as well?"

sentences.zh: 太子繇是遂敗。

translations.literal: The heir from this thereupon was ruined.
translations.idiomatic: From that moment the heir's cause was lost.

sentences.zh: 語在《戾園傳》。

translations.literal: The words are in the 'Biography of the Li Memorial Park.'
translations.idiomatic: The story is told in the biography of the Li heir's park.

sentences.zh: 後武帝知充有詐，夷充三族。

translations.literal: Later Martial Emperor knew Chong had deceived, exterminated Chong's three clans.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu saw the fraud, he extirpated Jiang Chong's three lineages.

sentences.zh: 息夫躬

translations.literal: Xifu Gong.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong.

sentences.zh: 息夫躬字子微，河內河陽人也。

translations.literal: Xifu Gong, style Ziwei, was a man of Heyang in Henei.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong, courtesy Ziwei, was a native of Heyang in Henei commandery.

sentences.zh: 少為博士弟子，受《春秋》，通覽記書。

translations.literal: In youth he was a doctoral disciple, received the Spring and Autumn Annals, broadly perused annals and documents.
translations.idiomatic: As a young doctoral student he mastered the Spring and Autumn Annals and read widely in history.

sentences.zh: 容貌壯麗，為眾所異。

translations.literal: His appearance was stalwart and handsome; the multitude marveled at him.
translations.idiomatic: He was tall and striking, and men stared wherever he went.

sentences.zh: 躬既親近，數進見言事，論議亡所避。

translations.literal: Gong having become intimate, repeatedly advanced to audience speaking affairs, in discussion and debate avoided nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Once admitted to the inner court he spoke his mind at every audience and pulled no punch in debate.

sentences.zh: 眾畏其口，見之仄目。

translations.literal: The multitude feared his mouth; seeing him they slanted their eyes.
translations.idiomatic: The court feared his tongue and looked away when he passed.

sentences.zh: 躬上疏歷詆公卿大臣，曰：「方今丞相王嘉健而蓄縮，不可用。

translations.literal: Gong submitted a memorial slandering in turn dukes and ministers, saying: 'At present Chancellor Wang Jia is strong yet hoards and shrinks—cannot be used.
translations.idiomatic: He filed a memorial running down the high ministers: "Chancellor Wang Jia is hale enough to serve yet curls up like a hedgehog—useless.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫賈延墮弱不任職。

translations.literal: Imperial clerk Grandee Jia Yan is collapsing weak and does not fill his post.
translations.idiomatic: Grandee of the imperial secretariat Jia Yan is soft clay in a soldier's gloves.

sentences.zh: 左將軍公孫祿、司隸鮑宣皆外有直項之名，內實□不曉政事。

translations.literal: General of the Left Gongsun Lu and Metropolitan Superintendent Bao Xuan all outwardly have the name of stiff necks, inwardly are actually dull and do not understand government affairs (one character is missing in the received text).
translations.idiomatic: General of the Left Gongsun Lu and Metropolitan Superintendent Bao Xuan pass for blunt men of principle, yet behind the pose they are plodders who know nothing of governing.

sentences.zh: 諸曹以下僕脩不足數。

translations.literal: Below the various bureaus, petty clerks are not worth counting.
translations.idiomatic: The ranks below them are not worth naming.

sentences.zh: 卒有強弩圍城，長戟指闕，陛下誰與備之？

translations.literal: If suddenly strong crossbows surround the city, long halberds point at the palace gate—whom will Your Majesty find to meet them?'
translations.idiomatic: If strong archers ring the capital and halberds point at your gates, who will stand at your side?"

sentences.zh: 如使狂夫嘄□於東崖，匈奴飲馬於渭水，邊竟雷動，四野風起，京師雖有武蜂精兵，未有能窺左足而先應者也。

translations.literal: If madmen raise the cry on the eastern cliffs, the Xiongnu water horses in the Wei River, the borderlands thunder-shaken, the four wilds wind-rising—even if the capital has martial wasp elite troops, there will be none able to spy the left foot and respond first (the text is damaged in one place).
translations.idiomatic: If rebels howl from the eastern hills while nomad riders water their horses at the Wei, if the frontier shudders like thunder and alarm sweeps the countryside, even Chang'an's picked troops will not know which way to step first.

sentences.zh: 軍書交馳而輻湊，羽檄重跡而押至，小夫心耎臣之徒憒眊不知所為。

translations.literal: Military documents cross-gallop and hub together; feathered dispatches double tracks and press arrival; petty men and heart-soft ministers' sort are dazed and blind not knowing what to do.
translations.idiomatic: Dispatch riders will pile orders at the gates, urgent signals will come two deep, and the soft men who fill your ministries will stare at one another without a plan.

sentences.zh: 其有犬馬之決者，仰藥而伏刃，雖加夷滅之誅，何益禍敗之至哉！」

translations.literal: Those who have dog-or-horse resolution will swallow poison and lie on the blade; though you add extermination punishments, what use toward the arrival of calamity and defeat!'
translations.idiomatic: The bravest among them will swallow poison or fall on their swords; even mass executions will not stave off ruin."

sentences.zh: 躬又言：「秦開鄭國渠以富國強兵，今京師土地肥饒，可度地勢水泉，廣溉灌之利。」

translations.literal: Gong also said: 'Qin opened the Zheng Guo canal to enrich the state and strengthen the army; now the capital region's land is fat and fertile—one can measure terrain and water springs, broadly extend irrigation's benefit.'
translations.idiomatic: He added that Qin built the Zheng Guo canal to feed its armies, and that the capital district was fertile enough for a similar project.

sentences.zh: 天子使躬持節領護三輔都水。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven ordered Gong to hold staff and oversee protect capital-region waters.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor gave him a staff and put him in charge of waterworks for the three approaches around the capital.

sentences.zh: 躬立表，欲穿長安城，引漕注太倉下以省轉輸。

translations.literal: Gong set markers, wished to bore through Chang'an city, lead transport canals to pour below the Grand Granary to save relay transport.
translations.idiomatic: He staked out a channel to cut through Chang'an itself and feed the Grand Granary by water, sparing the cart trains.

sentences.zh: 議不可成，乃止。

translations.literal: Deliberation could not succeed; then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Counselors blocked the plan and it died.

sentences.zh: 董賢貴幸日盛，丁、傅害其寵，孔鄉侯晏與躬謀，欲求居位輔政。

translations.literal: Dong Xian's noble favor daily flourished; the Ding and Fu clans envied his favor; Marquis of Kongxiang Yan plotted with Gong, wishing to seek occupying position and assisting government.
translations.idiomatic: As Dong Xian's star rose, the Ding and Fu factions grew jealous; Marquis Yan of Kongxiang conspired with Xifu Gong to seize a seat at the center of power.

sentences.zh: 會單于當來朝，遣使言病，願朝明年。

translations.literal: It happened the Chanyu should come to court; he sent envoys saying illness, wished to court next year.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu was due at court but pleaded illness and asked to defer his visit a year.

sentences.zh: 躬因是而上奏，以為「單于當以十一月入塞，後以病為解，疑有他變。

translations.literal: Gong thereupon submitted a memorial, thinking: 'The Chanyu ought in the eleventh month enter the passes; afterward he uses illness as excuse—suspect there is other change.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong seized the chance to memorialize: "The nomad chief should have entered the passes in the eleventh month; this sudden illness smells of treason."

sentences.zh: 烏孫兩昆彌弱，卑爰□強盛，居強煌之地，擁十萬之眾，東結單于，遣子往侍。

translations.literal: Wusun's two Kunmi are weak; the chief of Beilian is strong and flourishing, occupies strong Huang lands, embraces a hundred thousand multitudes, east joins the Chanyu, sent a son to go attend (the graph for the chief's name is partly missing in the received text).
translations.idiomatic: The two Wusun regents are weak while the Beilian chief waxes strong on the Huang steppe with a hundred thousand riders, has bound himself to the Chanyu in the east, and has sent a son to attend the nomad court."

sentences.zh: 如因素強之威，循烏孫就屠之跡，舉兵南伐，並烏孫之勢也。

translations.literal: If they ride the Qiang's growing might, follow Wusun Jiutu's trail, raise troops and strike south, that would merge Wusun into their power.
translations.idiomatic: If the nomads use the Qiang surge and copy Wusun Jiutu's path, marching south to swallow Wusun whole, the western tribes would fall under one yoke.

sentences.zh: 烏孫並，則匈奴盛，而西域危矣。

translations.literal: If Wusun is swallowed, then the Xiongnu grow mighty and the Western Regions face ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wusun is gone the Xiongnu dominate the steppe and the road west becomes a killing ground.

sentences.zh: 可令降胡詐為卑爰□使者來上書曰：『所以遣子侍單于者，非親信之也，實畏之耳。

translations.literal: One may order surrendered Hu to pose as envoys of the Beilian chief and submit a memorial saying: 'The reason I sent my son to wait on the Chanyu was not to trust him; truly I feared him.
translations.idiomatic: Send a turncoat Hu in the guise of the Beilian chief's envoy with a memorial reading, "I did not send my son to the Chanyu's court out of love; I sent him because I dared not refuse."

sentences.zh: 唯天子哀，告單于歸臣侍子。

translations.literal: Only may the Son of Heaven pity me, inform the Chanyu to return my minister's attendant son.
translations.idiomatic: I beg the Son of Heaven to take pity and order the Chanyu to send my boy home."

sentences.zh: 願助戊己校尉保惡都奴之界。』

translations.literal: I wish to assist the Colonel of the Garrison in guarding the border of Edu slave.'
translations.idiomatic: I will help the colonel of the garrison hold the line at Edu slave." (Edu slave is a place name on the frontier.)

sentences.zh: 因下其章諸將軍，今匈奴客聞焉。

translations.literal: Then circulate this memorial among the generals so that Xiongnu hostages in the capital hear it.
translations.idiomatic: Leak that memorial to every general and let the Xiongnu hostages in Chang'an spread the word.

sentences.zh: 則是所謂『上兵伐謀，其次伐交』者也。」

translations.literal: This is what is called: 'The finest strategy attacks the enemy's plans; the next attacks his alliances.'
translations.idiomatic: That is the art of war Sunzi praised: break their plots first, then their alliances."

sentences.zh: 書奏，上引見躬，召公卿將軍大議。

translations.literal: When the memorial went up, the sovereign led Gong to audience and summoned dukes, ministers, and generals for full debate.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor read the paper, brought Xifu Gong before the court, and called the high command into council.

sentences.zh: 左將軍公孫祿以為「中國常以威信懷伏夷狄，躬欲逆詐造不信之謀，不可許。

translations.literal: General of the Left Gongsun Lu held that 'the Middle Kingdom always wins the barbarians with awe and good faith; Gong would reverse treachery and forge faithless schemes—it cannot be allowed.
translations.idiomatic: General of the Left Gongsun Lu said, "China has always bound the barbarians with trust and prestige. Xifu Gong would replace that with lies; his plan must be refused."

sentences.zh: 且匈奴賴先帝之德，保塞稱蕃。

translations.literal: Moreover the Xiongnu rely on the late emperor's kindness, guard the passes, and style themselves vassals.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu still live on the late emperor's grace: they hold the frontier for us and call themselves subjects.

sentences.zh: 今單于以疾病不任奉朝賀，遣使自陳，不失臣子之禮。

translations.literal: Now the Chanyu, because of illness, cannot manage to attend court for congratulations; he sends envoys to explain himself—he loses nothing of the duty owed by son and minister.
translations.idiomatic: The Chanyu is ill and cannot travel for the New Year audience; he has explained himself through envoys and has broken no feudal courtesy.

sentences.zh: 臣祿自保沒身不見匈奴為邊境憂也。」

translations.literal: Your servant Lu wagers his life that until he dies he will not see the Xiongnu trouble the border.'
translations.idiomatic: I stake my life that the nomads will not threaten the passes while I draw breath."

sentences.zh: 躬掎祿曰：「臣為國家計幾先，謀將然，豫圖未形，為萬世慮。

translations.literal: Gong seized on Lu, saying: 'Your servant plans for the state before events stir, schemes for what is coming, maps what has not yet taken shape, and weighs the next ten thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong rounded on him: "I think generations ahead, read omens before they ripen, and guard a throne you cannot see."

sentences.zh: 而左將軍公孫祿欲以其犬馬齒保目所見。

translations.literal: Yet the General of the Left Gongsun Lu would guarantee the future with the few years left to a dog or horse.
translations.idiomatic: You would wager the empire on the short sight of an old warhorse."

sentences.zh: 臣與祿異議，未可同日語也。」

translations.literal: Your servant and Lu hold opposite views—they cannot be mentioned on the same day.'
translations.idiomatic: He and I are not even arguing in the same language."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「善。」

translations.literal: The sovereign said: 'Good.'
translations.idiomatic: "Well spoken," said the emperor— rashly.

sentences.zh: 乃罷群臣，獨與躬議。

translations.literal: Then he dismissed the ministers and deliberated alone with Gong.
translations.idiomatic: He sent the court away and closeted himself with Xifu Gong alone.

sentences.zh: 因建言：「往年熒惑守心，太白高而芒光，又角星□於河鼓，其法為有兵亂。

translations.literal: Thereupon Gong proposed: 'In recent years Mars lingered in the heart lodge, Venus rode high with a long tail, and the Horn star met the River Drum (one character is missing in the text)—by the canons of astrology these mean armed rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: He went on to cite omens: Mars had stalled in the heart constellation, Venus blazed with a drawn sword, and the Horn star touched the celestial drum—every signbook reads those as war.

sentences.zh: 是後訛言行詔籌，經歷郡國，天下騷動，恐必有非常之變。

translations.literal: After that false rumors and written divination circulated through commanderies and kingdoms; the realm seethed—I fear some extraordinary upheaval is coming.
translations.idiomatic: Since then forged prophecies have raced the provinces and the people are restless. Something terrible is gathering.

sentences.zh: 可遣大將軍行邊兵，敕武備，斬一郡守，以立威，震四夷，因以厭應變異。」

translations.literal: Dispatch the Grand General to drill the border hosts, tighten armor, execute one grand administrator to set an example, awe the four barbarians, and thereby smother the omens.'
translations.idiomatic: Send the Grand General to the frontier, sharpen weapons, strike off one governor as a warning, terrify the border tribes, and choke the ill luck before it blooms."

sentences.zh: 上然之，以問丞相。

translations.literal: The sovereign approved and put the question to the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor liked the idea and turned to Chancellor Wang Jia.

sentences.zh: 丞相嘉對曰：「臣聞動民以行不以言，應天以實不以文。

translations.literal: Chancellor Jia replied: 'Your servant has heard that the people are moved by deeds, not by words, and Heaven is answered with substance, not with show.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Jia answered, "The people follow action, not slogans; Heaven answers sincerity, not theater."

sentences.zh: 下民微細，猶不可詐，況於上天神明而可欺哉！

translations.literal: The common people are slight—even they cannot be tricked; how much less exalted Heaven and the spirits—could those be deceived!'
translations.idiomatic: You cannot fool the meanest peasant; do you imagine you can fool Heaven?"

sentences.zh: 天之見異，所以敕戒人君，欲令覺悟反正，推誠行善。

translations.literal: When Heaven sends portents it is to warn the ruler, wishing him to wake, return to rectitude, extend good faith, and do good.
translations.idiomatic: Omens are Heaven's memos to the throne: wake up, mend your ways, rule with mercy."

sentences.zh: 民心說而天意得矣。

translations.literal: When the people's hearts are pleased, Heaven's intent is obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Win the people and you have answered Heaven.

sentences.zh: 辯士見一端，或妄以意傅著星曆，虛造匈奴、烏孫、西羌之難，謀動干戈，設為權變，非應天之道也。

translations.literal: Rhetoricians seize on one clue or recklessly pin meaning to the almanac, invent crises among Xiongnu, Wusun, and western Qiang, plot to stir shields and spears, and spin crafty schemes—this is not the Way of answering Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Sophists clutch at single stars, invent Xiongnu and Wusun panics, and clamor for war—none of that answers Heaven's warning."

sentences.zh: 守相有罪，車馳詣闕，交臂就死，恐懼如此，而談說者雲，動安之危，辯口快耳，其實未可從。

translations.literal: When a governor truly sins, his cart races to the palace gate and he goes to execution with arms crossed—such terror; yet talkers say they would shake the secure with danger—clever tongues delight the ear, but in fact cannot be followed.
translations.idiomatic: A guilty governor races to the capital to die with folded arms—so deep runs fear of the law. Glib men who promise to save the throne by risking it are pleasant to hear and fatal to follow."

sentences.zh: 夫議政者，苦其諂諛傾險辯慧深刻也。

translations.literal: He who deliberates on policy suffers most from flattery, treacherous tilting, cunning cleverness, and pitiless harshness.
translations.idiomatic: Statecraft sickens when flatterers, plotters, clever cynics, and cruel men hold the floor.

sentences.zh: 諂諛則主德毀，傾險則下怨恨，辯慧則破正道，深刻則傷恩惠。

translations.literal: Flattery destroys the ruler's virtue; treacherous tilting breeds hatred below; cunning cleverness breaks the right Way; pitiless harshness wounds grace and kindness.
translations.idiomatic: Flattery rots the sovereign's character, intrigue turns the people against you, wit without wisdom wrecks the true path, and cruelty kills the trust that holds a dynasty up."

sentences.zh: 昔秦繆公不從百里奚、蹇叔之言，以敗其師，悔過自責，疾詿誤之臣，思黃髮之言，名垂於後世。

translations.literal: Formerly Duke Mu of Qin ignored Baili Xi and Jian Shu, defeated his army, then repented, blamed himself, drove off deceitful ministers, and heeded gray-haired counsel—his name still shines.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu of Qin once spurned Baili Xi and Jian Shu, lost an army, then humbled himself, banished liars, and listened to old men—history still honors him.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下觀覽古戒，反覆參考，無以先人之語為主。」

translations.literal: May Your Majesty study ancient warnings, weigh them again and again, and not let earlier speakers be your only masters.'
translations.idiomatic: Read the old warnings, sift every counsel twice, and do not let the last man who spoke own your policy."

sentences.zh: 上不聽，遂下詔曰：「間者災變不息，盜賊眾多，兵革之征，或頗著見。

translations.literal: The sovereign did not listen and issued an edict: 'Lately omens and disasters have not ceased, bandits multiply, and signs of war have begun to show.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor brushed Wang Jia aside and proclaimed, "Disaster follows disaster, rebels multiply, and the drums of war are sounding."

sentences.zh: 未聞將軍惻然深以為意，簡練戎士，繕修干戈。

translations.literal: I have not heard that the generals take this to heart, drill the troops, or repair shields and spears.
translations.idiomatic: Yet I hear no general drilling men or mending armor."

sentences.zh: 器用□惡，孰當督之！

translations.literal: Arms and gear are □ ruined—who will answer for it!'
translations.idiomatic: Our arsenals rot while no one is called to account." (The text is damaged in one graph.)

sentences.zh: 天下雖安，忘戰必危。

translations.literal: Though the realm is at peace, forgetting war invites peril.
translations.idiomatic: Peace itself is perilous when a state forgets how to fight.

sentences.zh: 將軍與中二千石舉明習兵法有大慮者各一人，將軍二人，詣公車。」

translations.literal: Let the Grand Minister together with officials at the two-thousand-shi rank each nominate one man skilled in military science and broad design; nominate two generals; send them to the public carriage gate.'
translations.idiomatic: The Grand General and every minister at two thousand shi shall each name one strategist and two field commanders and report to the talent office at once."

sentences.zh: 就拜孔鄉侯傅晏為大司馬衛將軍，陽安侯丁明又為大司馬票騎將軍。

translations.literal: He thereupon invested Marquis of Kongxiang Fu Yan as grand marshal and guard general, and Marquis of Yangan Ding Ming as grand marshal and swift cavalry general.
translations.idiomatic: That day he named Fu Yan of Kongxiang grand marshal and guard general and Ding Ming of Yangan grand marshal of swift cavalry.

sentences.zh: 是日，日有食之，董賢因此沮躬、晏之策。

translations.literal: That day there was a solar eclipse; Dong Xian therefore blocked Gong and Yan's strategy.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse followed the edict; Dong Xian used it to kill Fu Yan's and Xifu Gong's plan.

sentences.zh: 後數日，收晏衛將軍印綬，而丞相御史奏躬罪過。

translations.literal: Days later Yan's guard-general seals were seized while chancellor and chief clerk memorialized Gong's crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Within days Fu Yan was stripped of his command and the ministers indicted Xifu Gong.

sentences.zh: 上繇是惡躬等，下詔曰：「南陽太守方陽侯寵，素亡廉聲，有酷惡之資，毒流百姓。

translations.literal: The sovereign from this hated Gong and company and decreed: 'Grand Administrator of Nanyang, Marquis of Fangyang Chong, has never had a name for integrity; he is cruel by nature and poisons the people.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor turned on the whole cabal: "Zhu Chong of Fangyang, governor of Nanyang, is a known tyrant who has poisoned his district."

sentences.zh: 左曹光祿大夫宜陵侯躬，虛造許諼之策，欲以詿誤朝廷。

translations.literal: Left aide and grandee of splendid prosperity, Marquis of Yiling Gong, forged deceitful schemes to mislead the court.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong of Yiling spun lies to bait the throne."

sentences.zh: 皆交遊貴戚，趨權門，為名。

translations.literal: Both courted noble in-laws and rushed powerful gates for reputation.
translations.idiomatic: Both men traded on palace connections and chased the mighty for fame.

sentences.zh: 其免躬、寵官，遣就國。」

translations.literal: Strip Gong and Chong of office and send them home to their fiefs.'
translations.idiomatic: Dismiss them both and pack them off to their estates."

sentences.zh: 躬歸國，未有第宅，寄居丘亭。

translations.literal: Gong returned to his fief without a house and lodged at Qiu post station.
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong went home landless and slept in a roadside inn.

sentences.zh: 奸人以為侯家富，常夜守之。

translations.literal: Rogues thought a marquis's household must be rich and kept night watch on him.
translations.idiomatic: Thieves decided a marquis must still be rich and staked out the inn.

sentences.zh: 躬邑人河內掾賈惠往過躬，教以祝盜方，以桑東南指枝為匕，畫北斗七星其上，躬夜自被髮，立中庭，向北斗，持匕招指祝盜。

translations.literal: Gong's townsman, clerk Jia Hui of Henei, visited Gong and taught him the curse-thief rite: take a mulberry twig pointing southeast as a spoon, draw the seven stars of the Dipper on it; Gong at night loosened his hair, stood in the courtyard facing the Dipper, held the spoon, beckoned, pointed, and cursed the thief.
translations.idiomatic: A fellow townsman named Jia Hui showed him a charm against thieves: carve the Northern Dipper on a southeast-pointing mulberry twig, then at midnight stand disheveled in the yard, face the pole star, and curse the stalkers with the twig as a wand.

sentences.zh: 人有上書言躬懷怨恨，非笑朝廷所進，候星宿，視天子吉凶，與巫同祝詛。

translations.literal: Someone memorialized that Gong nursed resentment, mocked the court's appointments, watched the stars for the Son of Heaven's fortune, and joined shamans in cursing prayer.
translations.idiomatic: An informer charged him with sneering at promotions, casting the emperor's horoscope, and hiring witches to curse the throne.

sentences.zh: 上遣侍御史、廷尉監逮躬，系雒陽詔獄。

translations.literal: The sovereign sent attendant imperial clerks and a commandant of justice supervisor to arrest Gong and bind him in Luoyang's imperial jail.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent censors and jailers to take him to the Luoyang prison.

sentences.zh: 欲掠問，躬仰天大呼，因僵仆。

translations.literal: When they meant to torture him for confession, Gong looked up and screamed, then fell stiff.
translations.idiomatic: As the questioners raised the rods, Xifu Gong howled at the sky and dropped as if dead.

sentences.zh: 吏就問，雲咽已絕，血從鼻耳出。

translations.literal: Officers examined him; he said his throat was severed, blood flowing from nose and ears.
translations.idiomatic: They leaned close; he whispered that his throat had been cut, though blood only ran from nose and ears.

sentences.zh: 食頃，死。

translations.literal: Within a meal's time he was dead.
translations.idiomatic: He was gone before the porridge cooled.

sentences.zh: 黨友謀議相連下獄百餘人。

translations.literal: More than a hundred associates and fellow plotters were chained into prison.
translations.idiomatic: Over a hundred allies followed him into the cells.

sentences.zh: 躬母聖，坐祠灶禍詛上，大逆不道。

translations.literal: Gong's mother Sheng was convicted for praying at the kitchen god to curse the sovereign—great rebellion lacking the Way.
translations.idiomatic: His mother Sheng was judged for cursing the emperor before the hearth spirit—capital treason.

sentences.zh: 聖棄市，妻充漢與家屬徙合浦。

translations.literal: Sheng was exposed in the market; his wife and dependents were moved to Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng died in the marketplace; his wife and the rest of the household were exiled to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 躬同族親屬素所厚者，皆免廢錮。

translations.literal: Gong's kinsmen whom he had favored were all dismissed and barred from office.
translations.idiomatic: Every relative he had enriched was stripped of rank and forbidden to serve.

sentences.zh: 哀帝崩，有司奏：「方陽侯寵及右師譚等，皆造作奸謀，罪及王者骨肉，雖蒙赦令，不宜處爵位，在中土。」

translations.literal: When Emperor Ai died, officials reported: 'Marquis of Fangyang Chong and Master of the Right Tan all devised wicked plots; their guilt touched the royal bone and flesh; though they received amnesty, they ought not to hold rank in the central plain.'
translations.idiomatic: At Emperor Ai's death the ministry ruled that Zhu Chong, Tan of the right, and their circle had conspired against the imperial clan; even pardoned, they could not remain in the heartland.

sentences.zh: 皆免寵等，徙合浦郡。

translations.literal: All were stripped like Chong and removed to Hepu commandery.
translations.idiomatic: They were cashiered like Chong and banished to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 初，躬待詔，數危言高論，自恐遭害，著絕命辭曰：「玄雲泱鬱，將安歸兮！

translations.literal: At first, while awaiting appointment, Gong often spoke startling words and feared harm; he wrote a "severance of fate" lament: 'Black clouds bank and roll—where shall I turn!'
translations.idiomatic: Long before, while he was still a waiting scholar, Xifu Gong had penned a death song from fear of his own tongue: "Black vapor stacks the sky—where can I flee?"

sentences.zh: 鷹隼橫厲，鸞徘徊兮！

translations.literal: Hawks and falcons slash the wind; the phoenix wheels and hesitates!'
translations.idiomatic: "Hawks tear straight through the gale; the phoenix hangs back in the storm."

sentences.zh: □若浮□，動則機兮！

translations.literal: □ like floating □—move and the trigger springs!'
translations.idiomatic: "I drift like foam on a trigger sea—one twitch and the bolt flies." (Two characters are missing in the manuscript.)

sentences.zh: 叢棘手戔□棧棧，曷可棲兮！

translations.literal: Bramble thickets prick and jab □—how may one roost!'
translations.idiomatic: "Thorns close on every side—there is no branch to rest on." (The line is damaged.)

sentences.zh: 發忠忘身，自繞罔兮！

translations.literal: Loyalty spent and body forgotten—I have tangled my own snare!'
translations.idiomatic: "Loyalty has ruined me; I tied the net myself."

sentences.zh: 冤頸折翼，庸得往兮！

translations.literal: Neck wronged, wings broken—how may I reach that shore!'
translations.idiomatic: "Neck wrung, wings snapped—how can I ever cross?"

sentences.zh: 涕泣流兮萑蘭，心結□兮傷肝。

translations.literal: Tears fall among reeds; the heart knots □ and injures the liver.
translations.idiomatic: "Tears mix with the reeds; grief knots the liver." (One character is missing.)

sentences.zh: 虹蜺曜兮日微，孽杳冥兮未開。

translations.literal: Rainbows flare while the sun fades; calamity lies dark—no dawn yet.
translations.idiomatic: "Rainbows outshine the dying sun; disaster broods in endless dusk."

sentences.zh: 痛人天兮鳴呼，冤際絕兮誰語！

translations.literal: Heaven and man alike cry out in pain; wrong cuts me off—who will speak for me!'
translations.idiomatic: "Heaven and earth howl together; I am cut off—who will hear my case?"

sentences.zh: 仰天光兮自列，招上帝兮我察。

translations.literal: I lift my eyes to the light and declare myself; I summon High God to judge my cause.
translations.idiomatic: "I call the bright sky to witness and summon High God to read my heart."

sentences.zh: 秋風為我吟，浮雲為我陰。

translations.literal: The autumn wind moans for me; drifting clouds shade me.
translations.idiomatic: "Let autumn wind be my dirge and moving clouds my pall."

sentences.zh: 嗟若是兮欲何留，撫神龍兮□其須。

translations.literal: Alas, in such a case what would I stay for—stroking the divine dragon's □ whiskers?'
translations.idiomatic: "Why linger in such an hour—only to stroke the dragon's beard and burn my hand?" (One character is missing.)

sentences.zh: 游曠迥兮反亡期，雄失據兮世我思。」

translations.literal: I roam the vast waste—no day of return; the hero loses his footing—the age will pity me.'
translations.idiomatic: "I wander the endless dark with no way home; the champion loses his footing—let later ages pity me."

sentences.zh: 後數年乃死，如其文。

translations.literal: Several years later he died, just as his text had said.
translations.idiomatic: Years later he died exactly as his own poem foretold.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：仲尼「惡利口之覆邦家」，蒯通一說而喪三俊，其得不亨者，幸也。

translations.literal: The appraiser says: Confucius "hated glib tongues that overturned states and clans"—Kuai Tong with one persuasion cost three heroes; that he escaped the cauldron was luck.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Confucius despised clever talk that ruined kingdoms; Kuai Tong's tongue cost three brilliant men their lives—his own escape from the pot was sheer fortune.

sentences.zh: 伍被安於危國，身為謀主，忠不終而詐讎，誅夷不亦宜乎！

translations.literal: Wu Pi dwelt at ease in a doomed state, himself chief plotter—loyalty not carried through but answered with deceit—was execution and wiping out not fitting!
translations.idiomatic: Wu Pi made himself chief strategist of a traitor king, then bought his life with betrayal—little wonder the axe found him.

sentences.zh: 《書》放四罪，《詩》歌《青蠅》，春秋以來，禍敗多矣。

translations.literal: The Documents banished the four criminals; the Odes sings "The Green Fly"—since Spring and Autumn, disaster from slander has been endless.
translations.idiomatic: The canon banished the Four Fiends and the Odes warned of green flies; from Spring and Autumn onward, slander has toppled more houses than war.

sentences.zh: 昔子□謀桓而魯隱危，欒書構□而晉厲弒。

translations.literal: Long ago a certain noble plotted for Duke Huan and Duke Yin of Lu stood in peril; Luan Shu framed a rival and Marquis Li of Jin was murdered (names partly missing in the text).
translations.idiomatic: A Lu prince plotted for Qi and left Duke Yin exposed; Luan Shu framed a minister and Duke Li of Jin died for it.

sentences.zh: 豎牛奔仲，叔孫卒；

translations.literal: Shu Niu drove Zhong to flight and Uncle Sun died;
translations.idiomatic: Shu Niu harried Zhong until Uncle Sun died;

sentences.zh: □伯毀季，昭公逐；

translations.literal: □ Bo slandered Ji and Duke Zhao was driven out (one name is missing).
translations.idiomatic: A noble slandered Ji, and Duke Zhao fled Lu. (The manuscript is damaged.)

sentences.zh: 費忌納女，楚建走；

translations.literal: Fei Ji married off a daughter and Prince Jian of Chu fled;
translations.idiomatic: Fei Ji married her daughter into the palace and Crown Prince Jian of Chu had to run;

sentences.zh: 宰嚭讒胥，夫差喪；

translations.literal: Zai Pi slandered Wu Zixu and Fuchai was lost;
translations.idiomatic: Zai Pi destroyed Wu Zixu and King Fuchai lost everything;

sentences.zh: 李園進妹，春申斃；

translations.literal: Li Yuan sent in his sister and Lord Chunshen perished;
translations.idiomatic: Li Yuan fed his sister to Lord Chunshen and buried him with her;

sentences.zh: 上官訴屈，懷王執；

translations.literal: Shangguan lodged a false charge and King Huai of Chu was seized;
translations.idiomatic: Shangguan forged a plea and King Huai walked into a trap;

sentences.zh: 趙高敗斯，二世縊；

translations.literal: Zhao Gao ruined Li Si and the Second Emperor strangled himself;
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Gao broke Li Si and the Second Emperor died on the silken cord;

sentences.zh: 伊戾坎盟，宋痤死；

translations.literal: Yi Li forged the covenant and Song Chou died;
translations.idiomatic: Yi Li faked a blood oath and Heir Zhu of Song died for it;

sentences.zh: 江充造蠱，太子殺；

translations.literal: Jiang Chong invented gu and the crown prince was killed;
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Chong planted dolls and the heir died;

sentences.zh: 息夫作奸，東平誅；

translations.literal: Xifu Gong worked treachery and the prince of Dongping was executed;
translations.idiomatic: Xifu Gong schemed and the prince of Dongping went to the block;

sentences.zh: 皆自小覆大，繇疏陷親，可不懼哉！

translations.literal: All began in small malice and overturned great houses—outsiders who ruined kin by slander—can one not fear!
translations.idiomatic: Each began as a petty stranger's lie that swallowed a throne—can such lessons not terrify?

sentences.zh: 可不懼哉！

translations.literal: Can one not fear!
translations.idiomatic: Who could face such a list and not tremble?
